text:
You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "l".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "l".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "a".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "l".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "a".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "l".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "a".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "l".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "l".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "a".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "l".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "l".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "l".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "l".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "l".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "l".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "a".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "l".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "a".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "l".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "l".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "a".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".

Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "l".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "a".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "l".

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "l".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "a".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "l".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "l".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "l".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "a".

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "l".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "l".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "l".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "l".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "a".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "l".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "a".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

You are taking part in a Dutch visual lexical decision experiment. On each trial, two short vertical fixation lines appear above and below the centre of the screen. Five hundred milliseconds later, a letter string appears in lowercase between the fixation lines. Your task is to decide as quickly and accurately as possible whether the letter string is a real Dutch word or a nonword (a pronounceable letter string that is not a Dutch word, e.g. 'flummol').
If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "a".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "l".
The letter string disappears as soon as you respond, or after 2,000 ms (whichever comes first). There is then a 500 ms blank interval before the next trial. Try to keep your accuracy above 85% across the block.

If the letter string is a real Dutch WORD, press the button "l".
If the letter string is a NONWORD, press the button "a".
